summary: High‑energy Warfield set with scorching Scarlet>Fire and tight jams
blurb: The second set shines with a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain and an inspired Estimated Prophet. Jerry trades phrases with Brent during the jam, while Bob adds vocal flair in Estimated Prophet. Overall the show delivers strong improvisations and solid songcraft.
review: On February 17, 1982 the Dead turned the Warfield into a furnace of improvisational fire. The first set opened with a rollicking New Minglewood Blues that flowed into Sugaree and a spirited Bird Song, setting an upbeat tone. It was in the second set where the night truly ignited: Scarlet Begonias segued seamlessly into Fire on the Mountain, launching a high‑octane jam that had Jerry feeding off Brent’s keyboard textures and even pausing to salute his phrasing. The band kept the momentum rolling through a soaring Estimated Prophet, an expansive Eyes of the World, and a tight rhythm section that drove Space into a rare “The Tiger” solo before closing with The Wheel and a jubilant Not Fade Away. Bob Weir’s playful vocal touches—especially his scatting on Estimated Prophet—added humor, while Phil Lesh’s bass provided a deep, resonant undercurrent despite occasional calls for more early‑set presence. In short, the Warfield show captures the Dead at a creative peak: fierce jams, tight interplay, and enough surprise moments to keep any Deadhead smiling.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Seven out of nine recordings describe the performance in positive terms, repeatedly praising the Scarlet>Fire sequence, Estimated Prophet jam, and overall high energy. No consistent negative comments appear, just two mixed reviews about recording edits. The strong, repeated acclaim warrants a +1.6 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard capture with clear mix, minimal tape‑flip artifacts, and the most complete set representation.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain jam ignites second set.
Jerry’s interplay with Brent and rare "The Tiger" solo in Space.
Strong improvisations on Estimated Prophet and Eyes of the World.
Solid rhythm section keeping the energy high throughout.

band_performance.Jerry: On fire throughout, especially during Scarlet>Fire and Estimated Prophet; pauses to appreciate Brent’s phrasing and slips in a rare "The Tiger" solo in Space.
band_performance.Phil: Provides deep bass foundation; mixed comments about early‑set presence but praised for his later contributions on Loser and overall groove.
band_performance.Bob: Adds vocal humor, scatting on Estimated Prophet and strong vocal outro on the jam, keeping the mood lively.
band_performance.Brent: Locks in with Jerry, delivering textured keyboard lines that drive the jams and support the rare solo moment.
